---
title: "Speed up with GPUs"
description: "Coming soon — GPU acceleration via rapids-singlecell."
categories: [openpipeline_rapids]
order: 60
---

[`openpipeline_rapids`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_rapids}<span class="swatch openpipeline_rapids"></span>

:::{.callout-important}
## Coming soon
`openpipeline_rapids` is not released yet. This page previews what it will offer;
the components and reference will land here once the package is published.
:::

The **`openpipeline_rapids`** package will provide GPU-accelerated versions of
compute-heavy steps via
[rapids-singlecell](https://rapids-singlecell.readthedocs.io/). On a
CUDA-capable GPU, steps like PCA, neighbor-graph construction, UMAP, and Leiden
clustering run substantially faster than their CPU equivalents.

## When it will help

GPU acceleration pays off on large cohorts (hundreds of thousands of cells and
up), where the dimensionality-reduction and clustering steps dominate wall-clock
time. For small datasets the CPU workflows are simpler and fast enough.

## How it will fit in

`openpipeline_rapids` pins the core `openpipeline` as a Viash dependency and
exposes drop-in GPU components. The pattern will be to run the standard
[processing](process-filter.qmd) and [integration](integrate.qmd) flow but
schedule the accelerated components onto a GPU (via a Nextflow profile / config
that requests a GPU-enabled compute environment).

:::{.callout-note}
Until the package is released, the [reference](../reference/index.qmd) shows no
`openpipeline_rapids` entries. Track progress on the package repo via Viash Hub.
:::

**Next:** [Tune compute resources →](tune-resources.qmd) ·
[reference →](../reference/index.qmd)
